title: Cyclosorus ornatipes
taxonomy: Cyclosorus ornatipes is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or thelypterid ferns. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, a large and diverse group of ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is a relatively small group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. These ferns are typically found in humid environments, such as near streams or in shaded areas. The classification of Cyclosorus ornatipes is still evolving, but it is generally accepted as a member of the family Thelypteridaceae.

intro: Cyclosorus ornatipes, also known as the ornate lace fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The ornate lace fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus ornatipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the ornate lace fern is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its wound-healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
